source: Invested by Aleph — Semiconductors, AI, global warming, geopolitics
summary: Global warming is a solved problem due to solar and battery economics; the world will run on sunlight within one to two generations
summary_extended: Baker argues that global warming is effectively a solved problem from an economic standpoint, driven by the relentless annual cost declines in solar energy and battery storage, which make renewables increasingly cheaper than all competing energy sources. He supports this view by noting that US per-capita emissions have already returned to approximately 1920 levels, and that China's aggressive solar and EV buildout was motivated by national energy security (lacking domestic fossil fuels) rather than ideology, demonstrating that economic and strategic incentives alone are sufficient to drive decarbonization. Baker credits Elon Musk and Tesla specifically with doing more for decarbonization than all climate activists combined, framing the transition as an economic inevitability rather than a policy outcome. He identifies battery storage manufacturing capacity as the key remaining bottleneck to full transition, and views fusion energy as additional long-term optionality on top of the solar-and-battery base case. The investment implication is a long-term structural view that the world will run predominantly on sunlight within one to two generations, though Baker names no specific equities in this segment of the thesis.
detail: Baker argues solar costs decline every year while all other energy sources get more expensive. US is back to ~1920 per-capita emissions levels. China's solar/EV push was driven by national security (no domestic fossil fuels), not ideology. Fusion as additional upside optionality. Elon/Tesla did more for decarbonization than all activists combined. Battery storage manufacturing capacity is the key remaining bottleneck.
confidence_evidence: Baker states 'I genuinely believe global warming is a solved problem' and 'the world's going to run on sunlight' with economic reasoning
quote_fragment: I genuinely believe global warming is a solved problem
